jokes.text: Man, we sure used to try harder back then.
jokes.setup: Nostalgic moment about their first delivery to the moon
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission they were lazy even in the past
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment that subverts nostalgia with self-aware laziness

jokes.text: A cherished family keepsake for hours to come.
jokes.setup: Traditional sales pitch about keepsakes lasting generations
jokes.punchline: 'Hours to come' instead of years - balloon will pop quickly
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expectations with perfect economy of language

jokes.text: Five years of art school.
jokes.setup: Fry compliments the balloon artist's skill
jokes.punchline: Expensive education wasted on making balloon animals
jokes.explanation: Relatable joke about education vs. career reality, delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: Ow!
jokes.setup: Balloon artist working on sculpture
jokes.punchline: Balloon pops in their face, immediate consequence of their 'skill'
jokes.explanation: Basic slapstick payoff to the art school setup, predictable but functional

jokes.text: Want a corn dog? Sure. Then your mom should have bought you one.
jokes.setup: Appears to be offering corn dog
jokes.punchline: Cruel rejection after raising hopes
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with a mean carnival worker - captures the setting well

jokes.text: The Mecha-Hexadecapus is about to flail erratically into action.
jokes.setup: Dramatic announcement of carnival ride
jokes.punchline: 'Flail erratically' - not exactly confidence-inspiring ride description
jokes.explanation: Absurd ride name combined with honest but alarming description

jokes.text: Poor Leela. I never even told her I loved her.
jokes.setup: Fry mourns not expressing love to apparently dead Leela
jokes.punchline: Sets up Bender's correction about the 140 times
jokes.explanation: Sets up the punchline well, very character-specific delusion

jokes.text: What! You told her, like, 140 times. Yeah, but she paid very little attention to me.
jokes.setup: Bender corrects Fry's false claim about never saying 'I love you'
jokes.punchline: Fry's pathetic admission that she ignored him anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bender's bluntness meets Fry's self-pity

jokes.text: And in this moment, Leela's dead.
jokes.setup: Bender giving life advice about living in the moment
jokes.punchline: Applies the wisdom in the darkest possible way
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Bender line that perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: The good news is, we found your stuffed animal. What's the bad news? Your wife is dead. She wasn't my wife. Oh, then there is no bad news.
jokes.setup: Classic good news/bad news structure
jokes.punchline: Technical correction makes death irrelevant
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with dark humor - bureaucratic indifference to tragedy

jokes.text: Seeing Leela fly off the Hexadecapus and crash through the moon dome and survive inside a stuffed animal by breathing a balloon was a dose of reality.
jokes.setup: Fry describing what opened his eyes to reality
jokes.punchline: Lists the most unrealistic sequence imaginable as his 'dose of reality'
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - completely absurd events called 'reality'

jokes.text: I'm kind of busy. ... Come in.
jokes.setup: Fry asking to come in and talk to Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender claims to be busy but immediately lets him in after hearing the dramatic context
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's performative indifference cracking when gossip potential is revealed

jokes.text: Uh-huh. Good talk. We're done here.
jokes.setup: Fry pours his heart out about proposing
jokes.punchline: Bender dismisses it like a boring business meeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender dismissal of emotional moment with corporate speak

jokes.text: My little meatbags growing up.
jokes.setup: Bender watching Fry leave after the proposal talk
jokes.punchline: Combines parental pride with robotic derogatory term 'meatbags'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line combining affection with his robotic disdain

jokes.text: You were outside the button ball, so you don't remember a thing I said.
jokes.setup: Professor realizes others don't remember his demonstration
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the time loop mechanics while explaining to audience
jokes.explanation: Clever way to handle time travel exposition while being funny about it

jokes.text: It fell off a man. The man got into a taxi, but the money did not.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg explaining how he found $10
jokes.punchline: Overly formal description of simple accident
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg speaking pattern - formal language for mundane events

jokes.text: Friends, I found $10. You found some money? Show me.
jokes.setup: Previous scene where Zoidberg found $10
jokes.punchline: Exact repetition but with Bender's different reaction showing time loop
jokes.explanation: Effective time loop demonstration through repetition with variation in Bender's response

jokes.text: It fell off a chump.
jokes.setup: Same money story repeated due to time loop
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's characterization of the money's owner gets meaner
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing character evolution in repeated scenario

jokes.text: Each time the button sends you back 10 seconds, it takes 10 seconds to recharge.
jokes.setup: Amy asking if you could go back indefinitely by pressing repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Professor's explanation creates a perfect limitation that makes the device useless for that purpose
jokes.explanation: Smart sci-fi writing that creates logical limitations, preventing plot holes

jokes.text: Hell, no. This button is for scientific use only.
jokes.setup: Amy asking about using the button for romantic moments
jokes.punchline: Professor's stern rejection of frivolous use despite obvious potential
jokes.explanation: Professor's typical academic stuffiness about practical applications of his inventions

jokes.text: Classy choice, chief. She'll crap her pants.
jokes.setup: Fry picks out an engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Crude expression mixed with classy sales talk
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between fancy jewelry store setting and crude language

jokes.text: I'll have what she's having.
jokes.setup: Leela gets shocked and falls over at Fry's proposal
jokes.punchline: Classic 'When Harry Met Sally' reference to someone's dramatic reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic pop culture reference applied to electrocution - predictable but effective

jokes.text: Oh, my gosh. This is all so sudden after 13 years.
jokes.setup: Leela responding to marriage proposal
jokes.punchline: Calls it sudden despite their 13-year relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic observation about their eternally slow relationship

jokes.text: Strange things happen when the shadows lengthen.
jokes.setup: Fry shows the janitor the time button
jokes.punchline: Ominous supernatural warning from building janitor
jokes.explanation: Unexpected mystical wisdom from mundane character - good foreshadowing

jokes.text: I traveled a thousand years forward in time, but without Leela, I have no future.
jokes.setup: Fry contemplating suicide when Leela doesn't show
jokes.punchline: Poetic juxtaposition of literal time travel with emotional truth
jokes.explanation: Beautifully constructed line that works on multiple levels - literal and emotional

jokes.text: Of course. My watch is fast because it kept running every time I hit the button.
jokes.setup: Fry realizes his timing was wrong
jokes.punchline: Clever explanation of time travel mechanics through mundane object
jokes.explanation: Smart resolution to time travel paradox with practical logic

jokes.text: I'm stuck in a loop. This isn't the moment I wanted to last forever.
jokes.setup: Fry wanted to make a special moment last forever
jokes.punchline: Instead he's trapped in the worst possible moment - falling to his death
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic callback to his earlier wish with dark consequences

jokes.text: We'd be shredded across the time-space continuum like human coleslaw.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining dangers of leaving time shelter
jokes.punchline: Horrific death compared to mundane food side dish
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comparison - cosmic horror reduced to cafeteria food

jokes.text: Yum.
jokes.setup: Professor describes being shredded like coleslaw
jokes.punchline: Bender's appreciative reaction to the food comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender reaction - focuses on food while ignoring death

jokes.text: I wasn't gonna tell, because I don't like being helpful. But I do like ratting people out, so, it was Fry.
jokes.setup: Bender has information about who stole the time button
jokes.punchline: His motivation is purely about enjoying betrayal, not helping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - won't help but loves causing trouble

jokes.text: Hooray. I'm trapped in a tiny fun room with friends.
jokes.setup: Everyone realizes they're stuck in the time shelter
jokes.punchline: Bender's sarcastic enthusiasm about confinement
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender sarcasm about being stuck with people he tolerates

jokes.text: At least I have Leela's comforting scream to look forward to.
jokes.setup: Fry falling to his death repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Finds comfort in his girlfriend's horrified reaction to his death
jokes.explanation: Darkly romantic in a twisted way - finding love even in horror

jokes.text: My boyfriend gave me a diamond
jokes.setup: Amy trying to help while moving quickly through time loops
jokes.punchline: Irrelevant personal information during crisis rescue
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate oversharing during inappropriate moment

jokes.text: Cups!
jokes.setup: Professor appearing dead after time manipulation
jokes.punchline: Random exclamation about cups while apparently deceased
jokes.explanation: Absurd final word that's both random and somehow fitting

jokes.text: But you killed the Professor. Yeah, but it worked. I died. I'm dead. I'm gonna die.
jokes.setup: Confusion about the Professor's death/resurrection
jokes.punchline: Professor's confused timeline of his own death states
jokes.explanation: Funny temporal confusion about death states in different timelines

jokes.text: He sure has a lot of blood for a skinny guy.
jokes.setup: Fry is dying/dead from falling
jokes.punchline: Bender's clinical observation about blood volume vs body mass
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - detached scientific curiosity about friend's gruesome death

jokes.text: You know those dreams where you're falling, and right before you hit the ground you wake up? Those are great.
jokes.setup: Watching Fry die repeatedly from falling
jokes.punchline: Wistful appreciation for the luxury of waking up from falling dreams
jokes.explanation: Clever contrast between dream falling and actual repeated death

jokes.text: Okay, I'm gonna run out there. Then, you guys form a human bus and crash into me at 40 miles an hour.
jokes.setup: Need to deploy airbag to save falling Fry
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously violent plan described matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd rescue plan with specific vehicular details

jokes.text: First, I'm gonna get back these earrings I loaned Amy three years ago.
jokes.setup: Asked what they should do in a frozen universe
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes petty personal grievance over cosmic crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - practical person focuses on mundane issues

jokes.text: I would marry you even if you weren't the last man on Earth.
jokes.setup: Fry proposes in frozen world where they're alone
jokes.punchline: Romantic declaration that subverts the cliché about 'last man on Earth'
jokes.explanation: Beautiful romantic line that cleverly inverts the common phrase in context

jokes.text: Do I, Turanga Leela, take you, Philip J. Fry, to be my lawful wedded husband? Uh, yes? Absolutely.
jokes.setup: Leela performing both sides of wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Fry's uncertainty about his own answer to marrying her
jokes.explanation: Cute self-doubt at his own wedding - very Fry character moment

jokes.text: If it keeps bugging us, we'll either kill it or adopt it.
jokes.setup: Mysterious glimmer keeps appearing
jokes.punchline: Two extremely opposite solutions presented as equally reasonable
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly casual presentation of murder vs adoption as equivalent options

jokes.text: And across those oceans.
jokes.setup: Fry mentions walking up 240 flights of stairs for champagne
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of crossing oceans as additional minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Understated reference to their vast travels in frozen world

jokes.text: Boy, did somebody hit you two with an old-and-ugly stick.
jokes.setup: Professor emerges after decades to find aged Fry and Leela
jokes.punchline: Variation on 'ugly stick' joke but specifying their age
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor insensitivity with a twist on familiar insult

jokes.text: I guess it's good we didn't have children.
jokes.setup: Learning they can reset the universe and forget everything
jokes.punchline: Relief about not having kids who would cease to exist
jokes.explanation: Dark but practical consideration of cosmic reset's family implications

jokes.text: Lefty loosey, righty tighty
jokes.setup: Professor fixing the universe-destroying time button
jokes.punchline: Uses basic handyman mnemonic for cosmic repair
jokes.explanation: Mundane solution to cosmic problem - reduces universe to basic mechanics

jokes.text: What? I could've fixed it that easily? You?
jokes.setup: Fry realizes the complex time problem had a simple solution
jokes.punchline: Professor's incredulous response to Fry thinking he could fix anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic - Professor's disdain for Fry's abilities
